recalls.Description: Dresbe is recalling its LED Christmas Headbands due to a serious risk of injury or death from battery ingestion. These novelty headbands were sold in a two-pack, featuring a red headband with candy cane decorations and a green headband with a Christmas tree design. Each headband contains three button cell batteries that power the LED lights, and the packaging is labeled with "HB-052" and "DRESBE LED Christmas Headband (2pc)."
recalls.Title: Dresbe LED Christmas Headbands Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Dresbe via email at dresberecall@shineprom.com for assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled Dresbe Christmas Headbands (with candy cane and Christmas tree attachments)
Recalled Dresbe Christmas Headbands (with lights)
Label affixed to the recalled headband package

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from March 2024 through June 2025 for approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Wenxi Xuefeng Technology Co., Ltd, dba Dresbe, of China

Hazards.Name: The headbands pose a risk of battery ingestion, as children can easily access the button cell batteries. This violates safety standards and lacks the necessary warnings under Reese's Law, potentially leading to serious injuries or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the headbands immediately and reach out to Dresbe for disposal instructions. A photograph of the disposed product must be emailed to dresberecall@shineprom.com to receive a refund. Note that button cell batteries are hazardous and should be disposed of following local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: Dresbe recalls LED Christmas Headbands due to a battery ingestion risk. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Kitosun has issued a recall for its Submersible LED Lights due to a significant risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion. These lights come equipped with 10 color-changing LEDs, two remote controls, and a total of 20 preinstalled CR2450 lithium batteries in the lights, along with four CR2032 lithium batteries in the remotes. Each light measures approximately 3.5 cm in diameter. It is crucial to note that button cell and coin batteries are hazardous and should be disposed of according to local hazardous waste regulations.
recalls.Title: Kitosun Submersible LED Lights Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risk from Battery Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Kitosun via email at sales@kitosun.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kitosun Submersible LED Lights
Recalled Kitosun Submersible LED Lights

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through November 2025 for approximately $23.

Distributors.Name: Kitosun Original Company Limited, dba Kitosun, of China

Hazards.Name: The LED lights violate safety standards for products containing button cell or coin batteries, as they have easily accessible lithium coin batteries that pose an ingestion risk to children. Additionally, they lack the necessary warnings mandated by Reese's Law. Ingested batteries can lead to serious injuries, internal chemical burns, and even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately keep the recalled lights out of children's reach and contact Kitosun for a full refund or a free replacement. To process the refund or replacement, consumers will need to send a photo of the product in the trash to gumilius@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Kitosun recalls Submersible LED Lights due to battery ingestion risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: DR Power is recalling its 62-volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO rechargeable lithium-ion battery packs used in various battery-powered products, including lawnmowers, trimmers, and snow throwers. The recall affects stand-alone battery packs with model number 414240, as well as removable battery packs included with outdoor equipment models 524340, SB11022XEN, T4X3026XEN, T4X3062XENR, CE75021XEN0, and CE77021XEN0. The model number can be found on the battery pack's label, which also displays the LiPRO and DR Power logos on its front and side panels. It is crucial not to dispose of these recalled batteries in regular trash or recycling bins due to their fire hazard potential. Instead, contact your local municipal household hazardous waste (HHW) collection center for proper disposal methods.
recalls.Title: DR Power Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery Packs Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DR Power at 800-454-8643 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@drpower.com, or visit https://drpower.com/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: DR Power LiPRO 62-volt 5.0 Ah rechargeable lithium-ion battery packs

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Model 414240 Battery Pack (5 Ah)
DR Power Model 524340, DR Trimmer Mower
DR Power Model SB11022XEN, DR Pulse 62V Snow Thrower
DR Power Model T4X3062XEN, DR Trimmer Pulse
DR Power Model CE75021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21"
DR Power Model CE77021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21" SP

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of incidents of fire and/or overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Ningbo New Team Import & Export Co. Ltd. ("Lera"), of China

Retailers.Name: Available at home improvement and hardware stores nationwide, as well as online at DRPower.com and CountryHomeProducts.com from April 2018 through July 2024, priced between $300 and $700 for outdoor equipment including battery packs, and $250 for individual battery packs.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of short-circuiting and ignition, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled battery packs and reach out to DR Power for a pro-rated refund based on the battery's age. Proof of destruction is required, which involves submitting two photographs as per the instructions on the recall website, along with completing the online recall form at https://www.drpower.com/5AHB-recall. If opting for disposal, it should align with local and state regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls lithium-ion battery packs due to fire risks. Learn how to dispose of them safely and get a refund.

recalls.Description: AliExpress is recalling RGB-branded LED strip lights that are powered by button batteries due to a significant risk of ingestion. These color-changing and dimmable lights, measuring approximately 0.4 inches, were available in various colors including white, warm white, blue, green, red, yellow, purple, and pink. The design allows easy access to the lithium coin batteries, posing a danger to children. Additionally, the products lack the necessary safety warnings as mandated by federal regulations.
recalls.Title: AliExpress Recalls RGB LED Strip Lights Due to Serious Ingestion Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to AliExpress through their online service at https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/home?language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp, email us_product_recall@aliexpress.com, or visit https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/knowledge?categoryId=1000110454&language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp&questionId=1061297473&regulation=US-product-recall for more information regarding the recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled AliExpress LED strip light - purple
Recalled AliExpress LED strip lights
Recalled AliExpress LED strip light - green

Retailers.Name: Available on AliExpress.com from March 2024 to November 2024 for approximately $3.

Distributors.Name: Dongguan Changsheng Heng Electronic Commerce Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to easy access to lithium coin batteries, leading to potential serious injuries and violations of federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must immediately cease using the recalled LED strip lights and contact AliExpress for a full refund. Additionally, they should destroy the lights by cutting them in half and properly dispose of them. A photo of the destroyed lights should be sent to us_product_recall@aliexpress.com or in response to the recall email from AliExpress. Note that button cell and coin batteries are hazardous and should be disposed of according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: AliExpress recalls RGB LED strip lights due to ingestion hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: Greater Goods is recalling its Digital Kitchen Scales due to safety violations regarding coin batteries. The affected models include 0480, 0455, 0456, 0458, 0473, 0479, 0481, 0747, 0748, 0749, 0751, 0752, and 0754. These scales, available in various colors such as gray, black, blue, green, pink, red, silver, and white, have a label with the brand and model on the back. The Greater Goods logo is prominently displayed on the front panel, and each scale comes with a pre-installed lithium CR3032 coin battery along with a spare battery in the packaging.
recalls.Title: Greater Goods Recalls Digital Kitchen Scales Due to Ingestion Risks Linked to Coin Batteries
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Greater Goods at 866-991-0397 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at recall@greatergoods.com. More information can be found online at www.greatergoods.com by clicking the recall banner or visiting https://shop.greatergoods.com/pages/replaceyourbatterydoor.

Retailers.Name: Alexandra's Kitchen in Niskayuna, NY, as well as online at Amazon, Walmart, and Greater Goods

Importers.Name: Greater Goods LLC, of Saint Louis, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible lithium coin batteries

Remedies.Name: Free recall kit with a secure battery door, labeling, and updated manual

recalls.metaDescription: Greater Goods recalls Digital Kitchen Scales over ingestion hazards related to coin batteries. Find details and contact information here.

recalls.Description: Chervon North America has announced a recall of SKIL brand 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries manufactured before May 1, 2021. These batteries, identifiable by the markings "SKIL" and "PWRCORE 40," were sold individually under model number BY8708-00 and as part of kits with SKIL 40V Brushless Mowers. Only the battery itself is subject to this recall. Consumers can find the model number and manufacturing date code on the battery's nameplate near the terminals. The affected manufacturing date codes range from November 2019 to April 2021.
recalls.Title: Chervon North America Issues Recall for SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Chervon North America at 833-476-5325, available from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email BY8708-00recall@na.chervongroup.com or visit www.skil.com/recalls for further information.

Images.Caption: Recalled SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Battery, model BY8708-00
Nameplate located on housing of the recalled battery shows model number and manufacturing date code (first 3 digits of serial number)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 100 reports of thermal incidents involving the batteries including overheating, melting, smoking and fire. These included eight reports of minor burns and/or smoke inhalation and 49 reports of related property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's and various hardware and home improvement stores across the country, as well as online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Lowes.com from October 2019 through December 2024, priced around $170 for individual batteries and about $400 for combination kits with lawnmowers.

Importers.Name: Chervon North America Inc., of Naperville, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the batteries immediately and contact Chervon for a free replacement or refund. Consumers can register for the recall at www.skil.com/recalls, where a battery collection kit will be provided for returning the recalled battery.

recalls.metaDescription: Chervon recalls SKIL 40V 5.0Ah Lithium-Ion Batteries due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a replacement or refund.

recalls.Description: Yamaha Corporation of America has issued a recall for their PA-10 AC Power Adapters, which are identified by date codes from 2126 to 2319. The adapters are marked with the Yamaha logo on the front, while the date code can be found on the back. These adapters were included with five models of Yamaha analog mixing consoles—MG10, MG10X CV, MG10XU, MG10XUF, and MG12XUK—and were also sold separately as replacement parts. Only the PA-10 AC Power Adapter is affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Yamaha PA-10 AC Power Adapters Recalled for Shock and Electrocution Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the PA-10 AC Power Adapter immediately and reach out to Yamaha for a complimentary replacement. Contact Yamaha at 844-703-5446 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email at pa10recall@yamaha.com, or visit www.yamaha.com/us/support/pa10recall/ for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled PA-10 AC Power Adaptor
Back of the Recalled PA-10 AC Power Adaptor Showing Date Code
A crack can occur between the recalled PA-10 AC Power Adapter's upper and lower cases

Retailers.Name: Available at Guitar Center, Amazon.com, Sweetwater Sound, Sam Ash, Musicians Friend, B & H Foto & Electronics, American Musical Supply, and other authorized Yamaha retailers across the country; also sold directly by Yamaha online at www.usa.yamaha.com and www.shop.usa.yamaha.com/ from June 2021 through November 2023 for approximately $60.

Importers.Name: Yamaha Corporation of America, of Buena Park, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of electrical shock and electrocution due to potential separation of adapter casing, exposing electrical wiring.

Remedies.Name: Free replacement AC Power Adapter for consumers who purchased the defective adapter or a mixing console with it.

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls PA-10 AC Power Adapters due to shock hazards. Stop using immediately and contact Yamaha for a free replacement.
